title: androsace adfinis var. brigantiaca
intro: Androsace adfinis var. brigantiaca, also known as Brigantine rock jasmine or Alpine rose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Primulaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, where its roots and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Androsace adfinis var. brigantiaca include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve making infusions or decoctions from its roots and leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of ointments or salves.
